name: Baa Baa Black Sheep
source: Traditional melody - same as 'Twinkle Twinkle Little Star' and 'Alphabet Song', variant of the 18th-century French melody 'Ah! vous dirai-je, maman'. ABC notation found at abcnotation.com, melody structure confirmed via multiple piano tutorial sources.
notes: This is the traditional English nursery rhyme melody in C major. The melody follows the classic structure shared with 'Twinkle Twinkle Little Star': verse structure with paired phrases. Each line alternates between quarter notes and concludes with a half note. The song dates back to 1744 (Tommy Thumb's Pretty Song Book). The melody was famously arranged by Mozart in his 'Twelve Variations on Ah! vous dirai-je, maman'.
